root_text.an8.80:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā kammaṁ kattabbaṁ hoti.
root_text.an8.80:1.5: ‘kammaṁ kho me kattabbaṁ bhavissati. Kammaṁ kho pana me karontassa kāyo kilamissati. Handāhaṁ nipajjāmī’ti.
root_text.an8.80:1.6: So nipajjati, na vīriyaṁ ārabhati appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāya.
root_text.an8.80:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā kammaṁ kataṁ hoti.
root_text.an8.80:2.3: ‘ahaṁ kho kammaṁ akāsiṁ. Kammaṁ kho pana me karontassa kāyo kilanto. Handāhaṁ nipajjāmī’ti.
root_text.an8.80:2.4: So nipajjati, na vīriyaṁ ārabhati appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāya.
root_text.an8.80:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā maggo gantabbo hoti.
root_text.an8.80:3.3: ‘maggo me gantabbo bhavissati. Maggaṁ kho pana me gacchantassa kāyo kilamissati. Handāhaṁ nipajjāmī’ti.
root_text.an8.80:3.4: So nipajjati, na vīriyaṁ ārabhati appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāya.
root_text.an8.80:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā maggo gato hoti.
root_text.an8.80:4.3: ‘ahaṁ kho maggaṁ agamāsiṁ. Maggaṁ kho pana me gacchantassa kāyo kilanto. Handāhaṁ nipajjāmī’ti.
root_text.an8.80:4.4: So nipajjati, na vīriyaṁ ārabhati appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāya.
root_text.an8.80:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya caranto na labhati lūkhassa vā paṇītassa vā bhojanassa yāvadatthaṁ pāripūriṁ.
root_text.an8.80:5.3: ‘ahaṁ kho gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya caranto nālatthaṁ lūkhassa vā paṇītassa vā bhojanassa yāvadatthaṁ pāripūriṁ. Tassa me kāyo kilanto akammañño. Handāhaṁ nipajjāmī’ti.
root_text.an8.80:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya caranto labhati lūkhassa vā paṇītassa vā bhojanassa yāvadatthaṁ pāripūriṁ.
root_text.an8.80:6.3: ‘ahaṁ kho gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya caranto alatthaṁ lūkhassa vā paṇītassa vā bhojanassa yāvadatthaṁ pāripūriṁ. Tassa me kāyo garuko akammañño māsācitaṁ maññe. Handāhaṁ nipajjāmī’ti.
root_text.an8.80:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppanno hoti appamattako ābādho.
root_text.an8.80:7.3: ‘uppanno kho me ayaṁ appamattako ābādho atthi kappo nipajjituṁ. Handāhaṁ nipajjāmī’ti.
root_text.an8.80:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gilānā vuṭṭhito hoti aciravuṭṭhito gelaññā.
root_text.an8.80:8.3: ‘ahaṁ kho gilānā vuṭṭhito aciravuṭṭhito gelaññā. Tassa me kāyo dubbalo akammañño. Handāhaṁ nipajjāmī’ti.
root_text.an8.80:8.4: So nipajjati, na vīriyaṁ ārabhati appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāya.
root_text.an8.80:9.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā kammaṁ kattabbaṁ hoti.
root_text.an8.80:9.5: ‘kammaṁ kho me kattabbaṁ bhavissati. Kammaṁ kho mayā karontena na sukaraṁ buddhānaṁ sāsanaṁ manasi kātuṁ. Handāhaṁ paṭikacceva vīriyaṁ ārabhāmi appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāyā’ti.
root_text.an8.80:9.6: So vīriyaṁ ārabhati appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāya.
root_text.an8.80:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā kammaṁ kataṁ hoti.
root_text.an8.80:10.3: ‘ahaṁ kho kammaṁ akāsiṁ. Kammaṁ kho panāhaṁ karonto nāsakkhiṁ buddhānaṁ sāsanaṁ manasi kātuṁ. Handāhaṁ vīriyaṁ ārabhāmi appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāyā’ti.
root_text.an8.80:11.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā maggo gantabbo hoti.
root_text.an8.80:11.3: maggo kho me gantabbo bhavissati. Maggaṁ kho pana me gacchantena na sukaraṁ buddhānaṁ sāsanaṁ manasi kātuṁ. Handāhaṁ vīriyaṁ …pe…
root_text.an8.80:12.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā maggo gato hoti.
root_text.an8.80:12.3: ahaṁ kho maggaṁ agamāsiṁ. Maggaṁ kho panāhaṁ gacchanto nāsakkhiṁ buddhānaṁ sāsanaṁ manasi kātuṁ. Handāhaṁ vīriyaṁ ārabhāmi …pe…
root_text.an8.80:13.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya caranto na labhati lūkhassa vā paṇītassa vā bhojanassa yāvadatthaṁ pāripūriṁ.
root_text.an8.80:13.3: ahaṁ kho gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya caranto nālatthaṁ lūkhassa vā paṇītassa vā bhojanassa yāvadatthaṁ pāripūriṁ. Tassa me kāyo lahuko kammañño. Handāhaṁ vīriyaṁ ārabhāmi …pe…
root_text.an8.80:14.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya caranto labhati lūkhassa vā paṇītassa vā bhojanassa yāvadatthaṁ pāripūriṁ.
root_text.an8.80:14.3: ahaṁ kho gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya caranto alatthaṁ lūkhassa vā paṇītassa vā bhojanassa yāvadatthaṁ pāripūriṁ. Tassa me kāyo balavā kammañño. Handāhaṁ vīriyaṁ ārabhāmi …pe…
root_text.an8.80:15.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppanno hoti appamattako ābādho.
root_text.an8.80:15.3: uppanno kho me ayaṁ appamattako ābādho. Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ vijjati yaṁ me ābādho pavaḍḍheyya. Handāhaṁ paṭikacceva vīriyaṁ ārabhāmi …pe…
root_text.an8.80:16.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gilānā vuṭṭhito hoti aciravuṭṭhito gelaññā.
root_text.an8.80:16.3: ‘ahaṁ kho gilānā vuṭṭhito aciravuṭṭhito gelaññā. Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ vijjati yaṁ me ābādho paccudāvatteyya. Handāhaṁ paṭikacceva vīriyaṁ ārabhāmi appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāyā’ti.
root_text.an8.80:16.4: So vīriyaṁ ārabhati appattassa pattiyā anadhigatassa adhigamāya asacchikatassa sacchikiriyāya.

translation_text.an8.80:0.3: Grounds for Laziness and Arousing Energy
translation_text.an8.80:1.1: “Mendicants, there are eight grounds for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:1.3: Firstly, a mendicant has some work to do.
translation_text.an8.80:1.5: ‘I have some work to do. But while doing it my body will get tired. I’d better have a lie down.’
translation_text.an8.80:1.6: They lie down, and don’t rouse energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:1.7: This is the first ground for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:2.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has done some work.
translation_text.an8.80:2.3: ‘I’ve done some work. But while working my body got tired. I’d better have a lie down.’
translation_text.an8.80:2.4: They lie down, and don’t rouse energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:2.5: This is the second ground for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:3.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has to go on a journey.
translation_text.an8.80:3.3: ‘I have to go on a journey. But while walking my body will get tired. I’d better have a lie down.’
translation_text.an8.80:3.4: They lie down, and don’t rouse energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:3.5: This is the third ground for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:4.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has gone on a journey.
translation_text.an8.80:4.3: ‘I’ve gone on a journey. But while walking my body got tired. I’d better have a lie down.’
translation_text.an8.80:4.4: They lie down, and don’t rouse energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:4.5: This is the fourth ground for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:5.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has wandered for alms, but they didn’t get to fill up on as much food as they like, coarse or fine.
translation_text.an8.80:5.3: ‘I’ve wandered for alms, but I didn’t get to fill up on as much food as I like, coarse or fine. My body is tired and unfit for work. I’d better have a lie down.’
translation_text.an8.80:5.4: They lie down, and don’t rouse energy for achieving the unachieved, attaining the unattained, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:5.5: This is the fifth ground for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:6.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has wandered for alms, and they got to fill up on as much food as they like, coarse or fine.
translation_text.an8.80:6.3: ‘I’ve wandered for alms, and I got to fill up on as much food as I like, coarse or fine. My body is heavy, unfit for work, like I’ve just eaten a load of beans. I’d better have a lie down.’
translation_text.an8.80:6.4: They lie down, and don’t rouse energy for achieving the unachieved, attaining the unattained, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:6.5: This is the sixth ground for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:7.1: Furthermore, a mendicant feels a little sick.
translation_text.an8.80:7.3: ‘I feel a little sick. Lying down would be good for me. I’d better have a lie down.’
translation_text.an8.80:7.4: They lie down, and don’t rouse energy for achieving the unachieved, attaining the unattained, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:7.5: This is the seventh ground for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:8.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has recently recovered from illness.
translation_text.an8.80:8.3: ‘I’ve recently recovered from illness. My body is weak and unfit for work. I’d better have a lie down.’
translation_text.an8.80:8.4: They lie down, and don’t rouse energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:8.5: This is the eighth ground for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:8.6: These are the eight grounds for laziness.
translation_text.an8.80:9.1: There are eight grounds for arousing energy.
translation_text.an8.80:9.3: Firstly, a mendicant has some work to do.
translation_text.an8.80:9.5: ‘I have some work to do. While working it’s not easy to focus on the instructions of the Buddhas. I’d better preemptively rouse up energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.’
translation_text.an8.80:9.6: They rouse energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:9.7: This is the first ground for arousing energy.
translation_text.an8.80:10.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has done some work.
translation_text.an8.80:10.3: ‘I’ve done some work. While I was working I wasn’t able to focus on the instructions of the Buddhas. I’d better preemptively rouse up energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.’
translation_text.an8.80:10.5: This is the second ground for arousing energy.
translation_text.an8.80:11.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has to go on a journey.
translation_text.an8.80:11.3: ‘I have to go on a journey. While walking it’s not easy to focus on the instructions of the Buddhas. I’d better preemptively rouse up energy …’ …
translation_text.an8.80:11.4: This is the third ground for arousing energy.
translation_text.an8.80:12.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has gone on a journey.
translation_text.an8.80:12.3: ‘I’ve gone on a journey. While I was walking I wasn’t able to focus on the instructions of the Buddhas. I’d better preemptively rouse up energy …’ …
translation_text.an8.80:12.4: This is the fourth ground for arousing energy.
translation_text.an8.80:13.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has wandered for alms, but they didn’t get to fill up on as much food as they like, coarse or fine.
translation_text.an8.80:13.3: ‘I’ve wandered for alms, but I didn’t get to fill up on as much food as I like, rough or fine. My body is light and fit for work. I’d better preemptively rouse up energy …’ …
translation_text.an8.80:13.4: This is the fifth ground for arousing energy.
translation_text.an8.80:14.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has wandered for alms, and they got to fill up on as much food as they like, coarse or fine.
translation_text.an8.80:14.3: ‘I’ve wandered for alms, and I got to fill up on as much food as I like, coarse or fine. My body is strong and fit for work. I’d better preemptively rouse up energy …’ …
translation_text.an8.80:14.4: This is the sixth ground for arousing energy.
translation_text.an8.80:15.1: Furthermore, a mendicant feels a little sick.
translation_text.an8.80:15.3: ‘I feel a little sick. It’s possible this illness will worsen. I’d better preemptively rouse up energy …’ …
translation_text.an8.80:15.4: This is the seventh ground for arousing energy.
translation_text.an8.80:16.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has recently recovered from illness.
translation_text.an8.80:16.3: ‘I’ve recently recovered from illness. It’s possible the illness will come back. I’d better preemptively rouse up energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.’
translation_text.an8.80:16.4: They rouse energy for attaining the unattained, achieving the unachieved, and realizing the unrealized.
translation_text.an8.80:16.5: This is the eighth ground for arousing energy.
translation_text.an8.80:17.1: These are the eight grounds for arousing energy.”
